audit_status.source_base: Greek Revelation text tradition with Exodus/song/temple imagery; draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for seven last plagues, glass sea, song of Moses and Lamb, temple opened.

verses.restored: And I saw another sign in heaven, great and marvelous: seven messengers having seven last plagues, because in them wrath of Divine is completed.
And I saw as sea of glass mixed with fire, and those conquering from beast and from its image and from number of its name standing upon sea of glass, having harps of Divine.
They sing song of Moses servant of Divine and song of Lamb, saying: Great and marvelous are your works, Lord Divine Almighty; righteous/just and true are your ways, King of nations.
Who will not revere, Lord, and glorify your name? Because you alone are holy; all nations will come and worship before you, because your righteous/just acts were manifested.
After these things I saw, and temple of tent of witness in heaven was opened.
The seven messengers having seven plagues came out from temple, clothed in clean bright linen and girded around chests with golden sashes.
One of four living creatures gave to seven messengers seven golden bowls full of wrath of the Divine who lives into ages of ages.
The temple was filled with smoke from glory of Divine and from his power, and no one was able to enter temple until seven plagues of seven messengers were completed.

translation_notes: Song of Moses and Lamb links Exodus liberation and Lamb witness; preserve continuity.
Bowls complete wrath as symbolic judgment against beast/Babylon systems.
Nations come to worship; judgment is not anti-creation but toward manifest justice.
safety_note: Bowl imagery should be treated as liturgical/apocalyptic judgment symbolism, not as literal disaster prediction.
